question: Which attribute on a DMN `<output>` element holds a short, human-readable description of the output?

a.text: The docs state: "An output label is a short description of the output. It is set on the `output` XML element in the `label` attribute."

b.text: `name` is the reference used to access the output value (e.g. `decisionId.outputName`), not its human-readable description.

c.text: `id` is a unique technical identifier, not a description.

d.text: There is no `description` attribute on `<output>` in DMN. The label attribute serves that role.

explanation: The `label` attribute on `<output>` carries the column header text shown in the modeler. It is recommended but not required: omitting it leaves a valid decision table that is simply harder to read.
